Notice Of Motion And Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ara Jabagchourian, Esq. (650-437-6840) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Diane B. Sherman, Esq. (310-694-5132) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The matter before the Court is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFFS MARCUS HALL and DEAN MOREHOUS' Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Motion For Sanctions is granted and their request for monetary sanctions is denied. Within ten calendar days, defendant must provide a further verified response to special interrogatory 21 identifying all litigation and arbitration brought by former or current equity partners of defendant against defendant during the time period stated in the interrogatory and a full unredacted copy of the deposition of Mr. Novak taken in the Trost arbitration. Absent agreement by the parties or further order of the court, all such information may be used by plaintiffs only for this litigation and may be provided only to plaintiffs themselves, their counsel, and experts retained for this case. Monetary sanctions are denied because defendant's positions do not lack substantial justification. Court states findings as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. There has been sufficient showing that Novak Druce Connolly Bove & Quigg LLP's deposition may lead to discovery of admissible evidence. Written order is signed and filed in open court. The written order is stayed for 21 days to allow defendant to file a writ seeking a further stay of the order.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org. (D302)
